title: [InstCombine] Make backedge check in op of phi transform more precise
summary: The generalization replaced a reachability check with explicit backedge detection to prevent infinite combine loops when pushing operations into PHI nodes. Originally, `foldOpIntoPhi` used `isPotentiallyReachable` to block transformations that could move instructions across loop cycles, but this was overly conservative and expensive. The patch introduces a lightweight `BackEdges` cache computed once via reverse post-order traversal, identifying edges where the successor is already visited. This allows a precise, fast check (`isBackEdge`) to block transformations only when pushing an operation across a loop backedge, enabling the optimization in more cases (like PR32419) while avoiding infinite combines. The key insight: track loop structure directly rather than relying on generic reachability, balancing safety and optimization power.
